pattern: Reduce the plate to a letter-frequency requirement, then scan words keeping the shortest one whose own letter counts cover that requirement.
hints: Only the alphabetic letters of the plate matter, case-folded; digits and spaces are noise.
Build a count of how many times each letter the plate needs, then a word qualifies only if it has at least that many of every required letter.
Walk the words in order and remember the shortest qualifying one; ties keep the earlier word because you only replace on a strictly shorter length.

edge_inputs.description: needs two s plus t and p
tie on length returns the earliest word
single required letter, shortest containing word
case insensitivity, uppercase plate vs lowercase words
repeated requirement filters words with too few copies
plate full of non-letters needs nothing, shortest word wins
exact length match decided by coverage

stress.description: random plates and word lists with a guaranteed answer
stress.generator_python:
import string
def gen(rng, size):
    letters = 'abcde'
    need = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 3)))
    plate = need + ''.join(rng.choice('0123456789 ') for _ in range(rng.randint(0, 3)))
    words = []
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 200)))
    for _ in range(n):
        w = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 12)))
        words.append(w)
    # guarantee at least one completing word by appending the needed letters to a random word
    base = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(rng.randint(0, 5)))
    words.append(base + need)
    rng.shuffle(words)
    return [plate, words]
stress.note: always inserts a completing word so an answer exists
